question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of query timeout enforcement in findByFilter() to submit a filter that generates a pathologically slow DB query (e.g., complex join on unindexed columns), holding a DB connection indefinitely and preventing transaction confirmation queries from obtaining connections?
